Package i5.cb.graph

Class GraphEditor

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.awt.Component
      • java.awt.Container
        • java.awt.Window
          • java.awt.Frame
            • javax.swing.JFrame
              • i5.cb.graph.GraphEditor
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ILangChangeable, java.beans.PropertyChangeListener, java.util.EventListener

    public class GraphEditor
extends javax.swing.JFrame
implements ILangChangeable, java.beans.PropertyChangeListener
    The basic of all GraphEditor Components. The GraphEditor is the main Frame with a Menu, a toolbar and a status bar. You can add your own GraphInternalFrames to this object.
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Field Detail

      • currentLayoutDir

        protected java.io.File currentLayoutDir

      • m_graphToolBar

        protected javax.swing.JToolBar m_graphToolBar
        The editor's ToolBar. Usually contains save, open, new...etc buttons.

      • m_graphStatusBar

        protected javax.swing.JToolBar m_graphStatusBar

      • m_graphMenuBar

        protected GraphMenuBar m_graphMenuBar
        The editor's MenuBar. Usually contains file, edit, language,...etc menus.

      • m_statusProgressBar

        protected javax.swing.JProgressBar m_statusProgressBar
        A progressbar that is placed in the m_graphStatusBar. It's passed to the gifworker of the currently active GraphInternal Frame so the m_gifWorker can issue information concerning its progress

      • m_statusConnectionLabel

        protected javax.swing.JLabel m_statusConnectionLabel

      • s_cbgraphParams

        protected java.lang.String[] s_cbgraphParams
        The current command line arguments by which CBGraph was started or that are imported from the GEL file

      • m_vGraphInternalFrames

        protected java.util.Vector m_vGraphInternalFrames
        The GraphInternalFrames currently placed inside this GraphEditor window

    • Constructor Detail

      • GraphEditor

        public GraphEditor​(boolean bShowInfoWindow)
        Instanciates a new GraphEditor Object and specifies the bShowInfoWindow Flag. If the Constructor is called without a String specifying the Mainwindow's title, it is called with the default title "GraphEditor 0.5 b"
        Parameters:
        bShowInfoWindow - - shall the info window be shown?

      • GraphEditor

        public GraphEditor​(java.lang.String title)
        Creates a new GraphEditor instance with the specified title.
        Parameters:
        title - the title of the editor's mainWindow

      • GraphEditor

        public GraphEditor​(java.lang.String title,
                   boolean bShowInfoWindow,
                   boolean bShowGraphToolBar,
                   java.applet.Applet applet)
        Creates a new GraphEditor Object with a progressbar
        Parameters:
        title - specifies the windowtitle to be displayed on the mainwindow
        bShowInfoWindow - true will provide some space to show information about any object
        bShowGraphToolBar - true will show a toolBar (currently with no buttons)
        applet - applet of this grah editor instance (null if run as application)

    • Method Detail

      • setCBGraphParams

        public void setCBGraphParams​(java.lang.String[] newparams)
        Set the current CBGraph startup parameters; can come from the args of the cbgraph script call or from the GEL file; only set if the parameters specify the synchronization of module sources

      • getCBGraphParams

        public java.lang.String[] getCBGraphParams​()
        Get the current CBGraph startup parameters

      • setSubtitle

        public void setSubtitle​(java.lang.String subtitle)
        The GraphEditor frame title is of the form "CBGraph - ". This method updates the subtitle, e.g. by the name of the currently loaded graph file.

      • getToolBarButton

        public javax.swing.JButton getToolBarButton​(java.lang.String sHandle)
        Returns a button from the toolbar

      • addButtonToToolBar

        public javax.swing.JButton addButtonToToolBar​(java.lang.String pic,
                                              java.awt.event.ActionListener action,
                                              java.lang.String sHandle)
        Creates a JButton that performs the specified action on mouse click. If the pic parameter is the correct filename(incl.path) of a picture, an image is created. Else the button is labeled with the String. Currently the button is always added at the end of the toolBar.
        Parameters:
        pic - filename of an icon including the path
        action - the action that will be done on mouse click

      • addButtonToToolBar

        public javax.swing.JButton addButtonToToolBar​(java.lang.String pic,
                                              java.awt.event.ActionListener action,
                                              java.lang.String sHandle,
                                              java.lang.String sToolTipText)
        Creates a JButton that performs the specified action on mouse click. If the pic parameter is the correct filename(incl.path) of a picture, an image is created. Else the button is labeled with the String. Currently the button is always added at the end of the toolBar.
        Parameters:
        pic - filename of an icon including the path
        action - the action that will be done on mouse click
        sToolTipText - a TooltipText for the new Button

      • setInfoDoc

        public void setInfoDoc​(javax.swing.text.DefaultStyledDocument dInfo)
        Displays the specified document on the InfoPane.
        Parameters:
        dInfo - any DefaultStyledDocument - should not be null

      • addGraphInternalFrame

        public void addGraphInternalFrame​(GraphInternalFrame gif)
        Adds the given frame to this editor's desktopPane and creates a new GraphInternalFrameListener. If editor and GIF are visible, the frame is also set selected;
        Parameters:
        gif - the frame to be added

      • getGelFileForLoad

        public java.io.File getGelFileForLoad​(java.lang.String filename)
                               throws java.io.IOException
        Returns a the file specified by the given filename. If the filename is null, a file chooser dialogue is used to select a GEL file (type *.gel). This method is only called by loadGraphInternalFrame.
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException - if an error occurs

      • loadGraphInternalFrame

        public void loadGraphInternalFrame​()
                            throws java.io.IOException,
                                   java.lang.ClassNotFoundException
        calls loadGraphInternalFrame(.,.), i.e. forces the file chooser dialogue to select the GEL file, uses the currently active (selected) GraphInternalFrame as target for the load
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException
        java.lang.ClassNotFoundException

      • loadGraphInternalFrame

        public void loadGraphInternalFrame​(GraphInternalFrame frame,
                                   java.lang.String gelFilename)
                            throws java.io.IOException,
                                   java.lang.ClassNotFoundException
        Opens GEL file to create a FileInputStream. Replaces the title and the DiagramDesktop of the active Frame by the title and DiagramDesktop loaded from this stream. Expects one Frame to be selected. Keep in mind that there will not happen very much, if the GraphInternalFrame does not implement the loadUserObject Method. Loads the diagram desktop with the content stored in the GEL file. If the GEL file name is null, then a file chooser dialog is used to select it.
        Parameters:
        frame - the internal frame into which the content of the GEL file shall be loaded
        gelFilename - the name of the GEL file
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException
        java.lang.ClassNotFoundException

      • fitToScreen

        public java.awt.Dimension fitToScreen​(java.awt.Dimension d)
        Adapt the window size to the screen size.
        Parameters:
        d - original Dimension of a window to be displayed on the current screen
        Returns:
        Dimension that is the reduction of Dimension d to the screen size

      • fitToGE

        public java.awt.Dimension fitToGE​(java.awt.Dimension geSize,
                                  java.awt.Dimension gifSize)
        Adapt the window size of a graph internal frame to the size of the graph editor window-
        Parameters:
        geSize - size of the graph editor window
        gifSize - proposed size of the graph internal window
        Returns:
        Dimension that is the reduction of Dimension gifSize to the geSize

      • getActiveGraphInternalFrame

        public GraphInternalFrame getActiveGraphInternalFrame​()
        Returns:
        the active GIF, null if no GIF is selected.

      • countGIFs

        public int countGIFs​()
        Returns:
        the number of graph internal frames of this graph editor

      • getDesktopPane

        public javax.swing.JDesktopPane getDesktopPane​()
        Returns:
        the DesktopPane of the GraphEditor

      • getGraphToolBar

        public javax.swing.JToolBar getGraphToolBar​()
        Gets you the m_graphToolBar for editing
        Returns:
        the editor's toolbar

      • setGraphToolBar

        public void setGraphToolBar​(javax.swing.JToolBar graphToolBar)

      • getGEBundle

        public java.util.ResourceBundle getGEBundle​()
        The geBundle is sometimes needed for translation purposes
        Returns:
        this editor's textBundle

      • showProgressStatus

        public void showProgressStatus​(boolean bShowIt)
        Lets one controll if the program's progressbar shall be shown (if the gifworker wants to show something) or not. The method is used by the IFrameWorker of the currently active GraphInternalFrame, as we only want to see the progressbar while the m_gifWorker is working
        Parameters:
        bShowIt - a boolean value

      • getGraphMenuBar

        public GraphMenuBar getGraphMenuBar​()

      • getProgressBar

        public javax.swing.JProgressBar getProgressBar​()
        Returns the progressbar This is used to hand over the progressbar to a m_gifWorker so it can manipulate it thus showing its progress
        Returns:
        a JProgressBar value

      • updateLang

        public javax.swing.text.DefaultStyledDocument updateLang​(java.util.Locale loc)
        Sets this GraphEditor's Locale to the specified value and updates the MenuBar, the ToolBar and all GraphInternalFrames.
        Specified by:
        updateLang in interface ILangChangeable
        Parameters:
        loc - the new Locale
        Returns:
        always null at the moment

      • shutdown

        public void shutdown​()
        Shutdown this graph editor. If started as applet, the applet will be destroyed and disposed. If the graph editor was started as application, then it the application process is exited.

      • close

        public void close​()
        Close this graph editor. If it was not connected to a CBIva workbench, then a shutdown is performed. Otherwise, it disconnects from CBIva, dosposes its internal frames, and sets itself to invisible. If the graph was changed, the user conforms whether the changes should be saved to a file.

      • openNewFrame

        public void openNewFrame​()

      • isApplet

        public boolean isApplet​()

        Parameters:
        mainDesktopPane - New value of property m_mainDesktopPane.

      • setStatusString

        public void setStatusString​(java.lang.String sStatus)
        Supposed to be called only by m_GraphIntenalFrameListener or extensions of this

        sStatus - the new status we want to change to.
